doctrine: MCP is sight, not hands.
Runtime proof remains separate from code-intelligence proof.
No game launch unless the active contract explicitly allows it.
If a command assumes Bannerlord should not be running, run ForgeStop first.
Do not commit real local secrets, tokens, saves, or machine-specific config.
AGENTS.md is the safe bootloader; the v2 skill manifest routes conditional lane knowledge.
Select one primary skill and only declared required or composed skills.
Every skill declares entry authority, validators, expected artifacts, proof ceiling, risk class, freshness source, owned paths, and forbidden paths.
Mutable PR restrictions and active targets belong in current-state packets rather than AGENTS.md.
Local artifacts are evidence-bearing events. They do not parse, route, validate, or trigger themselves.
Automatic artifact engines remain deterministic and read-only; parser success may not be promoted into build, behavior, or runtime proof.
Known launcher windows are canonical metadata identities with tracked aliases and exact action policies; they are not unnamed pictures or coordinate targets.
Window lifecycle state is reduced deterministically from ordered events; rejected transitions never mutate the previous materialized state.
Action dispatch is a proof ceiling, not evidence that Bannerlord accepted the action.
Unknown windows enter quarantine and cannot acquire action authority until a tracked identity is explicitly resolved.
launcher-window-context.json is the sole authority for PLAY versus CONTINUE after the target PID and HWND are frozen.
The S1/S2 PID and window delta is a first-seen learning fallback, while image parsing remains diagnostic only.
Stale PRs are classified and selectively replayed, not blindly deleted, squashed, or used as bases.
Stale PR recovery is complete only when every planned source PR has an evidence-backed terminal disposition in the canonical progress ledger.
Continuum is an optional development accelerator, never a BlacksmithGuild build, validation, launch, or runtime dependency.
Composed end-to-end proof is the default merge target for executable, launcher, command, persistence, and integration changes.
AgentSwitchboard and SysAdminSuite may consume a sprint capsule only within the registered authority boundary and may not promote BlacksmithGuild proof.
Every serious app needs a repo-local AI harness. Prompts are one artifact inside it, not the harness itself.
The harness includes: agent rules, codebase map, workflow specs, run context, artifact registry, validators, hooks, skills, code intelligence, reports, and handoff compression.
Action-commitment: any prompt claiming to install, build, execute, repair, or release must produce the corresponding mutation and proof. Acknowledgment, plans, and summaries are not completion.
Escape menu (pause menu) is auto-dismissed by ClockResumeHelper via Win32 keybd_event. Operator intervention is no longer required for pause-menu recovery.

Engine health heartbeat: every engine that performs autonomous work must log a start token, periodic progress heartbeats, and a stop token (success or failure). A silent engine exit (Phase1.log stops without a stop token) is a crash until proven otherwise.

Vanilla mechanics over code injection: trade menus, taverns, horse markets, villages, and recruitment should follow in-game UI mechanics visually. Background analysis is fine; on-screen navigation must use game surfaces, not direct mutations.

Pause is not pause: escape menu (GUI overlay) blocks input and freezes automation. Time pause (campaign clock stopped) keeps the map open but stops time. Engines must distinguish the two: dismiss escape menu via keybd_event; resume time pause via TimeControlMode = StoppablePlay. Never assume one implies the other.

Travel before trade: market intel may be gathered anywhere, but settlement interior navigation (trade menu, tavern, horse market, smithy, recruitment) requires arrival at the target settlement. Do not probe settlement-specific APIs or InGameNotice the player about buy/sell before arrival. Route order: travel -> arrive -> enter settlement -> open trade menu -> execute trade.

Surface validation before dispatch: every command has a required game surface (map_surface, town_surface, settlement_menu, etc.). Dispatchers must verify the current surface before issuing commands. A travel command on a settlement menu or a trade command on the campaign map is a surface mismatch and must be rejected with machine-readable evidence. Test-TbgReadinessTrigger.ps1 enforces this with -RequiredSurface parameter.

DLL identity before runtime proof: the built DLL (Module/bin) and installed DLL (Modules/bin) sha256 must match. A running game may have loaded a stale DLL even if files match on disk. Test-TbgDllIdentity.ps1 checks file hashes and deploy timing. Runtime proof from a stale DLL is invalid.

Session context is machine-readable: every harness run declares -SessionMode (Human/Runner/FreshTestLaunch/UserSession/RunnerCleanup). A human playing the game differs from an automated test run. The session mode is recorded in the sprint capsule, readiness trigger output, and DLL identity check. Context distinguishes human observation from automated validation.

Every launch is identifiable: each forge launch generates a unique launch ID (forge-YYYYMMDDTHHmmssZ-xxxxxx) written to BlacksmithGuild_LaunchId.json. All harness artifacts (heartbeat, DLL identity, readiness trigger, sprint capsule) carry the launch ID. Concurrent launches are traceable; no two runs share an ID.

Multitasking is flagged: when the operator dictates or switches windows, the game may lose focus. The harness must detect focus loss, auto-dismiss pause menus, and continue running. Town scene buildings (trade district, tavern, keep, arena) are separate destinations within the settlement interior — entering the town does not enter a specific building. Traverse the 3D scene to reach target building before sending interact commands.

Focus before input: every keyboard/mouse command requires the game window to have foreground focus. Focus-TbgGameWindow.ps1 finds the Bannerlord window by title prefix via EnumWindows and calls SetForegroundWindow. Window title is 'Mount and Blade II Bannerlord - Singleplayer'. Never assume the game has focus after dictation or window switch.
